rationale: The citation is used for a specific functional role, not just background listing. In Study 2b, the authors explicitly build on perceptual fluency research and cite Novemsky et al. (2007) to support using hard-to-read versus easy-to-read fonts as a manipulation of experienced choice difficulty/effort. They then use that manipulation to test their own effort-compatibility predictions, so the cited paper meaningfully informs the citing paper's design and conceptual setup.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Novemsky et al. (2007)'s fluency-in-choice insight and font-based fluency manipulation to create exogenous variation in experienced effort in Study 2b. This borrowed mechanism is used to test a different focal theory (complicating vs. simplifying choice under effort-compatibility).
